nodes.name: Webhook
nodes.notes:
Pi-hole v6 — DNS sinkhole status and control. Use for checking DNS blocking stats, managing blocklists, and controlling Pi-hole.

Actions:
  status — overall Pi-hole stats including queries, blocked count, and client count. Use for "Pi-hole status", "how many queries blocked", "DNS stats".
  top_blocked — most frequently blocked domains. Use for "what domains are blocked most", "top blocked domains".
  top_clients — clients making the most DNS queries. Use for "which devices query most", "top DNS clients".
  enable — enable Pi-hole DNS blocking. Use for "enable Pi-hole", "turn on blocking", "resume blocking".
  disable — disable Pi-hole DNS blocking, optionally for a duration. Use for "disable Pi-hole", "pause blocking", "disable for 5 minutes".
  query_log — recent DNS queries with status. Use for "recent DNS queries", "what was queried", "DNS log".

Rules:
- disable accepts an optional duration in seconds. If omitted, blocking is disabled permanently until re-enabled.
- top_blocked and top_clients accept a limit parameter (default 10).
- query_log accepts a limit parameter (default 20).

---schema
{
  "action": {
    "type": "string",
    "description": "One of: status, top_blocked, top_clients, enable, disable, query_log",
    "required": true
  },
  "limit": {
    "type": "integer",
    "description": "Max items to return. Default 10 for top_blocked/top_clients, 20 for query_log. For: top_blocked, top_clients, query_log"
  },
  "duration": {
    "type": "integer",
    "description": "Seconds to disable blocking. Omit for permanent disable. For: disable"
  }
}

parameters.jsCode: return [{ json: { message: 'Pi-hole blocking enabled.', success: true } }];

parameters.content:
